title: Data Science MSc: Programme Overview
text: Learn about the latest developments in data science from active researchers who take you beyond textbooks. The comprehensive 1-year MSc Data Science programme spans topics from machine learning to big data and social data analytics to take you to becoming a skilled data scientist, using data to solve human challenges. The programme stands out for: Specialised knowledge and mastery in the powerful information landscape, that is highly valued in industry and government. A blend of sophisticated Big Data analytics, computer science and creativity involved in designing powerful visualisations. Large-scale analysis and research techniques working with real data Independent research and a dissertation on a subject of your interest. Networking opportunities across a larger research ecosystem (events, collaboration, exposure to industry-scale problems). Application of data techniques and tools in meeting emerging business needs and ambitious societal problems. The University of Southampton is a university partner of The Alan Turing Institute , the UK’s national institute for data science and AI. This partnership reflects our research strength and allows access to cutting-edge expertise and strong technical depth.
summary: Data Science MSc - Programme Overview: Learn about the latest developments in data science from active researchers who take you beyond textbooks. The comprehensive 1-year MSc Data Science programme spans topics from machine learning to big data and social data analytics to take you to ...

title: Data Science MSc: What You Will Study
text: The programme structure comprises 2 semesters of taught modules, culminating in a final semester independent (supervised) research project and written dissertation, demonstrating advanced analytical and research skills. Semester 1 focuses on key topics in foundational data science, machine learning, visualisation, and research methods for the postgraduate level. Semester 2 helps you to build on these foundations. After completing the taught component, you will conduct supervised independent research, yielding a master’s dissertation. The 60-credit major project (summer term) gives you space to develop your interests through an independent project. Course modules The set of first semester modules comprises Foundations of Data Science, Data Visualisation and Machine Learning Technologies to introduce core concepts, methods, techniques and tools. A fourth compulsory module prepares you for your final-semester dissertation project. The second semester modules include Data Mining, Social Media and Network Science, Natural Language Processing and Deep Learning Technologies and consolidate conceptual learning and practice using real-world data. Finally, the third semester research project gives you the chance to demonstrate your ability to design and deliver advanced applications, systems and solutions to a tight deadline through a substantial dissertation. For more information, see our Programme Description Document . Programme structure As a research-led University, we undertake a continuous review of our programmes to ensure quality enhancement and to manage our resources. As a result, this programme may be revised during a student’s period of registration; however, any revision will be balanced against the requirement that the student should receive the educational service expected. Please read our Disclaimer to see why, when, and how changes may be made to a student’s programme.
summary: Data Science MSc - What You Will Study: The programme structure comprises 2 semesters of taught modules, culminating in a final semester independent (supervised) research project and written dissertation, demonstrating advanced analytical and research skills. Semester 1 focuses on key t...

title: Data Science MSc: Key Information
text: We are mandated by the University Grants Commission (UGC) to deliver the equivalent programme and quality as we offer in the UK. To match the programme lengths of internationally respected UK awarded degrees, undergraduates study for three years, and postgraduates study for one year. View University of Southampton Delhi term dates . Dates mentioned are subject to change. Our programmes are taught in English, and content is equivalent to the MSc Data Science degree delivered at University of Southampton in the UK.
summary: Data Science MSc - Key Information: We are mandated by the University Grants Commission (UGC) to deliver the equivalent programme and quality as we offer in the UK. To match the programme lengths of internationally respected UK awarded degrees, undergraduates study for three years, ...

title: Data Science MSc: Your Future Career
text: We are a top 20 UK university for employability ( QS Graduate Employability Rankings 2022 ) and offer extensive support for today’s data-rich landscape. The programme emphasises problem-solving using advanced data science methods that you can then apply in diverse fields. Career opportunities Graduates commonly work in a range of organisations or sectors including Manufacturing, Scientific and Technical, Water and Waste Management, Education, Public Administration, Defence or Finance. Careers directly related to this course: Data analyst Data scientist Software engineer Machine learning engineer Big data engineer Research scientist Careers services at University of Southampton Delhi Our proprietary Southampton skills model is integrated into our curriculum. When you join us, you’ll be able to use our skills model to track, plan, and benefit your career development and progress. As well as support from the Careers and Employability team in Delhi, you’ll have access to our market-leading employability development offering. This includes: online resources regular workshops personalised career guidance and support through our MyCareer platform and e-mentoring With links to multinational and industrial organisations, we enable you to engage with employers and explore potential career paths. We also host regular alumni events in-person across major cities in India, allowing you to grow your own networks. Our support is ongoing, and once you graduate, you’ll be part of a global community of over 220,000 alumni that spans over 180 countries.
summary: Data Science MSc - Your Future Career: We are a top 20 UK university for employability ( QS Graduate Employability Rankings 2022 ) and offer extensive support for today’s data-rich landscape. The programme emphasises problem-solving using advanced data science methods that you can then...

title: Data Science MSc: Eligibility Criteria
text: The programme is primarily designed for students with a first degree in computing or a closely related degree covering fundamental notions of programming or data analysis. In order to apply for the programme, you require an upper second-class degree with a mark of 65% or above or international equivalent in computer science or a closely related subject: computer science computer engineering software engineering mathematics/statistics physics You must also have a good 2:1 (65%) score or equivalent in the following: 1 programming module (e.g. MATLAB, Python, Java, C, C++, C#, object-oriented or advanced programming language). 1 advanced maths module, ideally statistics and probability. English language requirement We accept several ways of meeting our English Language requirement. These include: IELTS Test, with scores of 6.5 overall (6.0 in reading, writing, speaking, and listening) other tests listed on our website an approved academic qualification having the required grade If you’ve successfully completed a degree, taught and assessed in English, up to 2 years before the date of first enrolment at the University of Southampton, we may be able to accept this as evidence of your English language ability. Refer to our English Language proficiency guide for more information about English language requirements. You may be invited for an in-person or online interview. We do not require that applicants appear for admission tests as part of the application process. Our academic admissions criteria Typical academic admissions criteria represent the usual level of academic attainment an applicant is expected to achieve prior to being admitted to that programme. These criteria are not intended to provide a guarantee that all applicants who satisfy the criteria may be offered a place, nor that all applicants to be made an offer will receive an identical offer to one another.

In order to apply for the programme, you require an upper second-class degr...

title: Data Science MSc: Fees and Costs
text: The fees for the year starting in August 2026 for Indian students are: 17,64,000 INR The tuition fee is paid in 4 instalments. Your first payment will be a deposit of 10% of the programme fee. To confirm your offer, you must pay your deposit as specified in your Conditional Offer. For students starting in August 2026, the deposit is: 1,76,400 INR for India students The balance of the yearly tuition fee is paid in 3 instalments. 4,23,360 INR by Monday 07 September 2026 5,82,120 INR by Monday 25 January 2027 5,82,120 INR by Monday 19 April 2027 Fees for international students The fees for the year starting in August 2026 for international students are: 20,16,000 INR The tuition fee is paid in 4 instalments. Your first payment will be a deposit of 10% of the programme fee. To confirm your offer, you must pay your deposit as specified in your Conditional Offer. For students starting in August 2026, the deposit is: 2,01,600 INR for international students If you are an international student, you’ll have to make 3 separate payments of: 4,83,840 INR by Monday 07 September 2026 6,65,280 INR by Monday 25 January 2027 6,65,280 INR by Monday 19 April 2027 We may adjust the fees in the future to account for inflation and changes in other costs. Exact payment dates for future years will vary according to the academic calendar. Scholarships We offer competitive scholarships to talented students.
summary: Data Science MSc - Fees and Costs: The fees for the year starting in August 2026 for Indian students are: 17,64,000 INR The tuition fee is paid in 4 instalments. Your first payment will be a deposit of 10% of the programme fee.

title: Data Science MSc: FAQ
text: Q: Can I get an offer with predicted scores? A: We encourage students to apply as soon as possible so that they can receive a Conditional Offer based on predicted scores for their chosen programme. We can accept an application from a student using their predicted grades if we receive their final grades once they are available.
summary: Data Science MSc - FAQ: Q: Can I get an offer with predicted scores? A: We encourage students to apply as soon as possible so that they can receive a Conditional Offer based on predicted scores for their chosen programme.
